name: Peleus
description: Peleus, son of Aeacus, King of Aegina, and Endeïs, was a legendary Thessalian hero in Greek mythology. He is best known as the husband of the sea nymph Thetis and the father of the famed hero Achilles. Peleus's life was marked by both great triumphs and significant tragedies. Early in his life, he and his brother Telamon were exiled from Aegina after killing their half-brother Phocus, driven by jealousy or accident depending on the account. He sought refuge in Phthia, where he was purified by King Eurytion. Peleus even assisted Eurytion in the Calydonian Boar hunt, but accidentally killed him during the event. Fleeing Phthia, he arrived in Iolcus, where he was purified by Acastus, the son of Pelias. However, Acastus's wife, Astydameia, attempted to seduce Peleus, and when he refused, she falsely accused him of trying to rape her. Acastus, unwilling to directly kill a guest, left Peleus unarmed on Mount Pelion, hoping the Centaurs would kill him. He was saved by Chiron, the wise Centaur, or by Hermes. Peleus eventually returned to Iolcus, killed Astydameia, and ravaged the city. His greatest achievement was winning the hand of Thetis, a sea nymph desired by both Zeus and Poseidon. However, a prophecy stated that Thetis would bear a son mightier than his father, deterring the gods. They were forced to marry her to a mortal, Peleus. Their wedding was a grand affair attended by all the Olympian gods, except Eris, who threw the Apple of Discord, triggering the events leading to the Trojan War. Peleus's significance lies not only in his own adventures but also in his role as the father of Achilles, the greatest Greek warrior of the Trojan War. He armed and mentored Achilles, guiding him towards glory and ensuring his son's immortality through heroic deeds.
subtitle: Father of Achilles, Thessalian Hero
achievements: Participated in the Calydonian Boar Hunt

keyMyths.title: The Calydonian Boar Hunt
keyMyths.description: Peleus joined the hunt for the Calydonian Boar, a monstrous creature sent by Artemis. During the hunt, he accidentally killed King Eurytion, forcing him to flee Phthia.

keyMyths.title: The Wedding of Peleus and Thetis
keyMyths.description: Peleus married the sea nymph Thetis, a wedding attended by all the Olympian gods. Eris, the goddess of discord, threw the Apple of Discord, sparking the events leading to the Trojan War.

keyMyths.title: Peleus and Astydameia
keyMyths.description: Astydameia, wife of Acastus, falsely accused Peleus of trying to seduce her after he rejected her advances. This led to Acastus abandoning Peleus on Mount Pelion.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive handbook of Greek mythology.
sources.text: A comprehensive handbook of Greek mythology.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: An epic poem focusing on the Trojan War, featuring Achilles, Peleus's son.
